content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

work on sd warren-368e. use ml. commit when done.

content.text:
pl-ad0f  approved  rev 2
Name:     Run Analytics dashboard (context-usage + agent-behavior mining)
Seed:     warren-a00a
Template: feature
Created:  2026-05-29T01:47:08.685Z
Updated:  2026-05-29T01:49:31.298Z
Review suggested (no reviewer recorded yet)

Sections:
  context
    Warren already ships a Cost Analytics dashboard, but the rich per-run logs (the `runs` token/cost columns plus the `events` table's `tool_use`/`tool_result` trace) are otherwise unmined. The operator wants a dedicated Run Analytics dashboard that answers behavioral questions the cost view can't: how much context (input + cache-read tokens) agents burn on average, which seeds consumed the most context, and — most importantly — where agents get stuck (commands they run repeatedly or that fail, e.g. `ml`, `sd`, `gh`/git, `bun run check:all`). Scouting confirmed the data is recoverable: per-run context lives in `runs.tokens_input/tokens_cache_read/tokens_output/cost_usd`; seed grouping works via `runs.seed_id` (seed-originated runs only); Bash commands land as `tool_use` events at `payload.input.command` with outcomes in correlated `tool_result` events (`is_error` + `content`, joined by `tool_use_id`). Known limitation: claude-code emits a single cumulative token total per run while pi emits per-turn, so per-turn growth curves are pi-only and per-run totals are the cross-runtime baseline.
  approach
    Mirror the existing Cost Analytics architecture exactly: pure, dialect-agnostic aggregator modules under `src/runs/analytics/` (each with a co-located `*.test.ts`), thin `RouteHandler` factories under a new `src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts` (keeping the grandfathered `lifecycle.ts` from growing), and a TanStack-Query UI page that leans on the existing shadcn components (`Card`, `Table`, `PageHeader`) plus the `CostAnalytics.tsx` filter pattern (`useSearchParams` project + date-range). Two endpoints under the already-registered `/analytics` prefix split the work by cost: `GET /analytics/runs` reads only the `runs` table (fast) for KPIs, time-series, per-agent/per-model breakdowns and top-seeds-by-context; `GET /analytics/behavior` scans `tool_use`/`tool_result` events (heavier, capped) for generalized command mining — ranking ALL commands by frequency/failure/retry with the os-eco toolchain (ml/sd/gh/bun check:all) highlighted — and a derived insights layer. Graphs use recharts (operator-approved new dependency) for time-series and bar charts; the bundle-size ratchet is bumped once with this tracker reference. Phase 1 ships run-level metrics + context + charts; Phase 2 layers behavioral command-mining + insights callouts on top.
  alternatives
    1. name: Tables/cards only, no chart library (clone Cost Analytics verbatim)
       rejected_because: Operator explicitly wants graphs; time-series context/run trends and per-agent comparisons read far better as charts than dense tables
    2. name: Single combined /analytics endpoint
       rejected_because: Event-table scanning for command mining is materially heavier than reading the runs table; splitting lets the fast run-level view render immediately and the heavy behavior view load/cap independently
    3. name: SQL-side aggregation with json_extract in the repo
       rejected_because: json_extract semantics differ between SQLite and the optional Postgres backend; following the cost-analytics precedent of fetching rows and aggregating in a pure, unit-tested TS function keeps it dialect-agnostic and testable without HTTP
    4. name: Hardcode only the os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun)
       rejected_because: Operator chose generalized ranking of all commands with os-eco ones highlighted, so emergent stuck-patterns outside the known toolchain are still surfaced
  steps
    1. Run-level analytics aggregator (src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts + run-metrics.test.ts)
       blocks: 2
    2. GET /analytics/runs handler (src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts) + ROUTE_TABLE entry + gen:docs/gen:openapi
       blocks: 4
    3. Add recharts dependency to src/ui + bump scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json ratchet (cite this tracker)
       blocks: 5
    4. UI API client + types for run analytics (src/ui/src/api/client.ts: runAnalyticsApi.runs)
       blocks: 5
    5. RunAnalytics page Phase 1: KPI cards + filters + recharts (runs-over-time, avg-context-per-agent, top-seeds-by-context, failure-reason) + per-agent/model tables; route in App.tsx + nav in Layout.tsx (reuse Card/Table/PageHeader, split sub-components under 500-line budget)
       blocks: 10, 11
    6. EventsRepo.listToolEventsForRuns(runIds) returning capped tool_use/tool_result rows (src/db/repos/events.ts)
       blocks: 9
    7. Command-mining aggregator (src/runs/analytics/command-mining.ts + test): parse tool_use input.command, generalized frequency/failure/retry ranking, correlate tool_result.is_error via tool_use_id, retry-loop + stuck-score, highlight os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun check:all)
       blocks: 8, 9
    8. Derived insights aggregator (src/runs/analytics/insights.ts + test): typed Insight[] callouts with severity (highest-context seed, worst-success agent, most-failed/most-retried command, model cost outlier, steering/pause anomalies)
       blocks: 9
    9. GET /analytics/behavior handler (src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts): scan capped events, return command insights + derived insights + ROUTE_TABLE entry + gen:docs/gen:openapi
       blocks: 10
    10. RunAnalytics page Phase 2: insights callout cards at top + command-category bar + stuck-command leaderboard table with os-eco highlighting (extend runAnalyticsApi.behavior, reuse Card/Table)
        blocks: 11
    11. Final quality gates + acceptance: aggregator unit tests, gen:docs, gen:openapi, coverage + bundle-size ratchets, bun run check:all
  risks
    1. Event-table scanning for command mining can be expensive on busy instances — cap rows per run / per query and constrain by the date-range filter, and keep /analytics/behavior separate from the fast /analytics/runs view
    2. claude-code emits only a single cumulative token total per run (pi emits per-turn), so per-turn context-growth charts are pi-only; the dashboard must present per-run totals as the cross-runtime baseline and label pi-only views
    3. token/cost columns are best-effort and null for agents that emit neither pi turn_end nor claude-code result — all aggregates must tolerate nulls and exclude them from averages rather than counting them as zero
    4. runs.seed_id is null for ad-hoc (non-seed) runs, so top-seeds-by-context must exclude nulls and the UI should note it covers seed-originated runs only
    5. Adding recharts trips check:deps/knip and check:bundle-size — recharts must be genuinely imported and the bundle-size budget bumped exactly once with this tracker reference (the ratchet otherwise only goes down)
    6. New ROUTE_TABLE entries drift docs/http-api.md and docs/openapi.yaml — gen:docs + gen:openapi must be regenerated and committed or check:all fails
    7. Bash command strings may contain secrets/tokens — the behavior endpoint and UI must surface commands without leaking sensitive argument values (truncate/redact long inline tokens)
  acceptance
    1. GET /analytics/runs returns KPIs (run count, success rate, avg/median/p95 duration, avg context tokens, avg/total cost), a runs-per-day time-series bucketed by state, per-agent and per-model/provider breakdowns, a failure-reason breakdown, and top-seeds-by-context — all honoring projectId + from/to filters and tolerating null token/cost rows
    2. The RunAnalytics page is reachable from the nav, mirrors the CostAnalytics project + date-range filter UX, and renders KPI cards plus recharts graphs (runs-over-time, avg-context-per-agent, top-seeds-by-context, failure-reason) using the existing Card/Table/PageHeader components
    3. GET /analytics/behavior ranks all observed commands by frequency/failure/retry, correlates failures via tool_result.is_error joined on tool_use_id, flags retry loops, highlights os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun run check:all), and is row-capped/date-bounded
    4. The page surfaces a derived insights callout section (e.g. highest-context seed, worst-success agent, most-failed/most-retried command) plus a command-category chart and a stuck-command leaderboard table with os-eco highlighting
    5. Each aggregator (run-metrics, command-mining, insights) has a co-located unit test exercising null-token, empty-input, and os-eco-highlight cases; coverage ratchet does not regress
    6. docs/http-api.md and docs/openapi.yaml are regenerated for the two new /analytics routes, recharts is declared and the bundle-size budget updated, and bun run check:all passes clean (lint, typecheck, tests, gen:*:check, check:deps, check:bundle-size, file-size, debt-markers, duplicates)

Children (12):
  warren-368e  [open]  Run-level analytics aggregator (src/runs/analytics/run-metrics.ts + run-metrics.test.ts)
  warren-0692  [open]  GET /analytics/runs handler (src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts) + ROUTE_TABLE entry + gen:docs/gen:openapi
  warren-876c  [open]  Add recharts dependency to src/ui + bump scripts/bundle-size-budgets.json ratchet (cite this tracker)
  warren-df6e  [open]  UI API client + types for run analytics (src/ui/src/api/client.ts: runAnalyticsApi.runs)
  warren-638a  [open]  RunAnalytics page Phase 1: KPI cards + filters + recharts (runs-over-time, avg-context-per-agent, top-seeds-by-context, failure-reason) + per-agent/model tables; route in App.tsx + nav in Layout.tsx (reuse Card/Table/PageHeader, split sub-components under 500-line budget)
  warren-e355  [open]  EventsRepo.listToolEventsForRuns(runIds) returning capped tool_use/tool_result rows (src/db/repos/events.ts)
  warren-8976  [open]  Command-mining aggregator (src/runs/analytics/command-mining.ts + test): parse tool_use input.command, generalized frequency/failure/retry ranking, correlate tool_result.is_error via tool_use_id, retry-loop + stuck-score, highlight os-eco commands (ml/sd/gh/bun check:all)
  warren-1788  [open]  Derived insights aggregator (src/runs/analytics/insights.ts + test): typed Insight[] callouts with severity (highest-context seed, worst-success agent, most-failed/most-retried command, model cost outlier, steering/pause anomalies)
  warren-5d50  [open]  GET /analytics/behavior handler (src/server/handlers/runs/analytics.ts): scan capped events, return command insights + derived insights + ROUTE_TABLE entry + gen:docs/gen:openapi
  warren-436a  [open]  RunAnalytics page Phase 2: insights callout cards at top + command-category bar + stuck-command leaderboard table with os-eco highlighting (extend runAnalyticsApi.behavior, reuse Card/Table)
  warren-9d89  [open]  Final quality gates + acceptance: aggregator unit tests, gen:docs, gen:openapi, coverage + bundle-size ratchets, bun run check:all
  warren-f062  [open]  Patch version bump for Run Analytics dashboard (package.json version + src/index.ts VERSION, keep in sync; add CHANGELOG.md entry) (adopted)

---states/reasons---
export const RUN_STATES = [
	"queued",
	"running",
	"paused",
	"succeeded",
	"failed",
	"cancelled",
] as const;
export type RunState = (typeof RUN_STATES)[number];

export const RUN_TERMINAL_STATES = [
	"succeeded",
	"failed",
	"cancelled",
] as const satisfies readonly RunState[];
export type RunTerminalState = (typeof RUN_TERMINAL_STATES)[number];

/**
 * Run mode discriminator (pl-0344 step 1 / warren-67b6). `batch` is the
 * historical single-shot run: warren spawns burrow, agent runs to completion,
 * reap pushes the branch. `interactive` is the respawn-per-turn primitive
 * powering brainstorm + planner agents (pl-0344 step 3 / warren-1117) — each
 * user message dispatches a fresh burrow turn that reads Plot context, replies,
 * and exits. Mode is fixed at run-create time. TS-only narrowing (mx-2ab984);
 * defaults to `batch` so legacy rows written before this column existed match
 * the historical shape.
 */
export const RUN_MODES = ["batch", "interactive"] as const;
export type RunMode = (typeof RUN_MODES)[number];

/**
 * Failure-cause discriminator for a `failed` run (warren-3c40, warren-5165).
 * `state:failed` alone can't tell several different failure shapes apart.
 * Reap infers from the warren state on entry plus event content:
 *
 *   - still `queued` on entry ⇒ no events ever flowed from burrow ⇒
 *     `never_started` (config/runtime issue, e.g. under-specified prompt).
 *   - `running` on entry but events table holds no model-turn output
 *     (`text` / `thinking` / `tool_use` on stdout) ⇒ `no_model_response`
 *     (typically a credential/auth failure — the original warren-5165
 *     symptom was claude-code emitting an init system event then exiting
 *     with "Not logged in" before any assistant turn — but also covers
 *     rate-limit and provider-network failures).
 *   - `running` on entry with model output ⇒ `crashed` (agent ran and
 *     hit an unrecoverable error mid-conversation).
 *   - `timed_out` is reserved for a future deadline-based reaper — burrow
 *     doesn't currently report a separate timeout state.
 *   - `burrow_run_lost` (warren-b1a9) means burrow returned 404 for the
 *     run's `burrow_run_id` — typically a warren-machine restart that
 *     wiped burrow's in-memory run state. The reconciler (bootBridges)
 *     and the bridge's mid-stream 404 catch both mark the warren row
 *     `failed` with this reason instead of looping forever.
 *   - `dropped_commit` (warren-72b9) means reap's `git push` landed zero
 *     commits ahead of the base branch (`reap.empty_push`) AND the
 *     workspace tree was still dirty at reap — the agent edited/staged
 *     work but never ran `git commit` (the common weak-model failure,
 *     e.g. Gemini Flash narrating "before committing" then exiting).
 *     Distinguished from a deliberate no-op (clean tree, zero commits)
 *     which stays `succeeded`. Marking the run `failed` keeps a dropped
 *     commit from masquerading as success and, on plan-runs, fails the
 *     plan instead of silently auto-merging/advancing past the child.
 *
 * Null on succeeded/cancelled rows.
 */
export const RUN_FAILURE_REASONS = [
	"never_started",
	"no_model_response",
	"crashed",
	"timed_out",
	"burrow_run_lost",
	"dropped_commit",
] as const;
export type RunFailureReason = (typeof RUN_FAILURE_REASONS)[number];

export const EVENT_STREAMS = ["stdout", "stderr", "system"] as const;
export type EventStream = (typeof EVENT_STREAMS)[number];

/**

content.thinking:
I'm mapping out the run-metrics aggregator endpoint that needs to return KPIs like run counts and success rates, time-series data bucketed by state, breakdowns by agent and model, failure reasons, and top seeds by context usage—all filtered by project and date range while handling missing token and cost data gracefully.

Defining the input row structure with run metadata and token counts, where context tokens combine input plus cache-read tokens, and duration is calculated from start and end timestamps when both exist. The output shape organizes totals with aggregated stats, time-series buckets by date, groupings by agent/model/provider, failure reason breakdowns, and top seeds ranked by context consumption.

Writing the TypeScript interfaces... Defining the context tokens calculation—a row only counts as having data if at least one of input or cache read tokens is present, then I sum them treating nulls as zero. I'll keep the module independent by defining its own NONE_KEY constant locally rather than importing it.
